sectiondef.header: System hang prevention Timeout counter value.

description.para: This value is used throughout the code to initialize a Timeout counter that is used when hard polling a register.
Definitions to be used with Endpoint related function that require a 'Direction' parameter.

NOTE: The direction is always defined from the perspective of the HOST! This means that an IN endpoint on the controller is used for sending data while the OUT endpoint on the controller is used for receiving data.
Definitions to be used with Endpoint related functions that require a 'Type' parameter.

briefdescription.para: Endpoint Max Packet Length in DeviceConfig is a coded value, ch9.6.6.

description.para: Definitions for the XUsbPs_GetStatus() function call 'StatusType' parameter.
Definitions that are used to identify events that occur on endpoints.
Passed to the endpoint event handler functions registered with XUsbPs_EpSetHandler().

briefdescription.para: Setup data has been received on the endpoint.
Data frame has been received on the endpoint.
Data frame has been sent on the endpoint.

sectiondef.header: dTD Next Link Pointer (dTDNLP) bit positions.

briefdescription.para: USB dTD Next Link Pointer Terminate Bit.
USB dTD Next Link Pointer Address [31:5].
USB dQH Interrupt on Setup Bit.
USB dQH Maximum Packet Length Field [10:0].
USB dQH Zero Length Termination Select Bit.

description.para: Register comments are tagged with "H:" and "D:" for Host and Device modes, respectively.
Tags are only present for registers that have a different meaning DEVICE and HOST modes. Most registers are only valid for either DEVICE or HOST mode. Those registers don't have tags.

briefdescription.para: Number of supported Endpoints in this core.
Mask used for endpoint control registers.

detaileddescription.para: Endpoint

description.para: These definitions are used by software to set the maximum rate at which the USB controller will generate interrupt requests.
The interrupt interval is given in number of micro-frames.

USB defines a full-speed 1 ms frame time indicated by a Start Of Frame (SOF) packet each and every 1ms. USB also defines a high-speed micro-frame with a 125us frame time. For each micro-frame a SOF (Start Of Frame) packet is generated. Data is sent in between the SOF packets. The interrupt threshold defines how many micro-frames the controller waits before issuing an interrupt after data has been received.

For a threshold of 0 the controller will issue an interrupt immediately after the last byte of the data has been received. For a threshold n>0 the controller will wait for n micro-frames before issuing an interrupt.
Therefore, a setting of 8 micro-frames (default) means that the controller will issue at most 1 interrupt per millisecond.

sectiondef.header: USB Interrupt Status Register (ISR) / Interrupt Enable Register (IER)

memberdef.name: XUSBPS_IXR_ALL
memberdef.initializer: (XUSBPS_IXR_UI_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_UE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_PC_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_FRE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_AA_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_UR_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_SR_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_SLE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_ULPI_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_HCH_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_RCL_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_PS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_AS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_NAK_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_UA_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_UP_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_TI0_MASK | \ XUSBPS_IXR_TI1_MASK)

sectiondef.header: USB Device Address Register (DEVICEADDR) bit positions.
USB TT Control Register (TTCTRL) bit positions.
USB Burst Size Register (BURSTSIZE) bit posisions.
USB Tx Fill Tuning Register (TXFILL) bit positions.
USB ULPI Viewport Register (ULPIVIEW) bit positions.
Port Status Control Register bit positions.

briefdescription.para: PHY Low Power Suspend - Clock Disable.

sectiondef.header: On-The-Go Status Control Register (OTGCSR) bit positions.

briefdescription.para: HW Assist Auto Reset Enable Bit.
HW Assist Data Pulse Enable Bit.
USB Hardware Assist B Disconnect to A Connect Enable Bit.
USB A VBus Valid Interrupt Status Flag.
USB A Session Valid Interrupt Status Flag.
USB B Session Valid Status Flag.
USB B Session End Status Flag.
USB A VBus Valid Interrupt Status Flag.
USB A Session Valid Interrupt Status Flag.
USB B Session Valid Interrupt Status Flag.
USB B Session End Interrupt Status Flag.
USB A VBus Valid Interrupt Enable Bit.
USB A Session Valid Interrupt Enable Bit.
USB B Session Valid Interrupt Enable Bit.
USB B Session End Interrupt Enable Bit.

memberdef.name: XUSBPS_OTG_ISB_ALL
memberdef.initializer: (XUSBPS_OTGSC_IDIS_MASK |\ XUSBPS_OTGSC_AVVIS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_ASVIS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_BSVIS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_BSEIS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_1MSS_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_DPIS_MASK)

memberdef.name: XUSBPS_OTG_IEB_ALL
memberdef.initializer: (XUSBPS_OTGSC_IDIE_MASK |\ XUSBPS_OTGSC_AVVIE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_ASVIE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_BSVIE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_BSEE_IEB_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_1MSE_MASK | \ XUSBPS_OTGSC_DPIE_MASK)

briefdescription.para: Mask for All IRQ status masks.
Each XUsbPs device in the system has an entry in this table.
Each XUsbPs device in the system has an entry in this table.
This function initializes a XUsbPs instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: Setup / Initialize functions.

The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XUsbPs structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

is a pointer to a XUsbPs_Config configuration structure. This structure will contain the requested configuration for the device. Typically, this is a local structure and the content of which will be copied into the configuration structure within XUsbPs.

is the base address of the device. For systems with virtual memory, this address must be the virtual address of the device. For systems that do not support virtual memory this address should be the physical address of the device. For backwards compatibility NULL may be passed in systems that do not support virtual memory (deprecated).

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE an error occurred during initialization.

simplesect.para: After calling XUsbPs_CfgInitialize() the controller IS NOT READY for use. Before the controller can be used its DEVICE parameters must be configured. See xusbps.h for details.

briefdescription.para: This function performs device reset, device is stopped at the end.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the USB device.

detaileddescription.para: Common functions used for DEVICE/HOST mode.

All the configuration registers are reset to their default values. The function waits until the reset operation is complete or for a certain duration within which the reset operation is expected to be completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

detaileddescription.para: Handling Suspend and Resume.

In order to conserve power, USB devices automatically enter the suspended state when the device has observed no bus traffic for a specified period. When suspended, the USB device maintains any internal status, including its address and configuration. Attached devices must be prepared to suspend at any time they are powered, regardless of if they have been assigned a non-default address, are configured, or neither. Bus activity may cease due to the host entering a suspend mode of its own. In addition, a USB device shall also enter the suspended state when the hub port it is attached to is disabled.

A USB device exits suspend mode when there is bus activity. A USB device may also request the host to exit suspend mode or selective suspend by using electrical signaling to indicate remote wakeup. The ability of a device to signal remote wakeup is optional. If the USB device is capable of remote wakeup signaling, the device must support the ability of the host to enable and disable this capability. When the device is reset, remote wakeup signaling must be disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has entered Suspend mode successfully

detaileddescription.para: If the USB controller is suspended, its operation is resumed when any non-idle signaling is received on its upstream facing port.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has Resumed successfully

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has Resumed successfully

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the controller's DEVICE address.

detaileddescription.para: It also sets the advance bit so the controller will wait for the next IN-ACK before the new address takes effect.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Address of the device.

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM: Invalid parameter passed, e.g. address value too big.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the endpoint pointer data structure.

detaileddescription.para: The function sets up the local data structure with the aligned addresses for the Queue Head and Transfer Descriptors.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.

simplesect.para: Endpoints of type XUSBPS_EP_TYPE_NONE are not used in the system. Therefore no memory is reserved for them.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Queue Head List in memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Transfer Descriptors lists in memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.

briefdescription.para: This function associates a buffer with a Transfer Descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: The function will take care of splitting the buffer into multiple 4kB aligned segments if the buffer happens to span one or more 4kB pages.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Transfer Descriptor
is pointer to the buffer to link to the descriptor.
is the length of the buffer.

listitem.para: XST_USB_BUF_TOO_BIG: The provided buffer is bigger than the maximum allowed buffer size (16k).

simplesect.para: Cache invalidation and flushing needs to be handler by the caller of this function.

briefdescription.para: This function set the Max PacketLen for the queue head for isochronous EP.

detaileddescription.para: If the max packet length is greater than XUSBPS_MAX_PACKET_SIZE, then Mult bits are set to reflect that.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.
is the Length to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function re-initializes the Queue Head List in memory.

detaileddescription.para: The endpoint 1 has been disabled before this call.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the updated XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.
The endpoint to be reconfigured.
The new transfer direction of endpoint 1

briefdescription.para: This function re-initializes the Transfer Descriptors lists in memory.

detaileddescription.para: The endpoint has been disabled before the call. The transfer descriptors list pointer has been initialized too.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.
The endpoint to be reconfigured.
The new transfer direction of endpoint 1

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, u8 EpNum, const u8 *BufferPtr, u32 BufferLen, u8 ReqZero)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_EpQueueRequest

briefdescription.para: This function sends a given data buffer and also sends ZLT packet if it is requested.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
is a pointer to the buffer to send.
is the Buffer length.
is the

listitem.para: XST_USB_BUF_TOO_BIG: Provided buffer is too big (>16kB).
XST_USB_NO_DESC_AVAILABLE: No TX descriptor is available.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the DEVICE side of the controller.

detaileddescription.para: The caller needs to pass in the desired configuration (e.g. number of endpoints) and a DMAable buffer that will hold the Queue Head List and the Transfer Descriptors. The required size for this buffer can be obtained by the caller using the: XUsbPs_DeviceMemRequired() macro.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that contains the desired DEVICE side configuration.

simplesect.para: The caller may configure the controller for both, DEVICE and HOST side.

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, u8 EpNum, const u8 *BufferPtr, u32 BufferLen)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_EpBufferSend

briefdescription.para: This function sends a given data buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
is a pointer to the buffer to send.
is the Buffer length.

listitem.para: XST_USB_BUF_TOO_BIG: Provided buffer is too big (>16kB).
XST_USB_NO_DESC_AVAILABLE: No TX descriptor is available.

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, u8 EpNum, const u8 *BufferPtr, u32 BufferLen)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_EpBufferSendWithZLT

briefdescription.para: This function sends a given data buffer and also zero length packet if the Bufferlen is in multiples of endpoint max packet size.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
is a pointer to the buffer to send.
is the Buffer length.

listitem.para: XST_USB_BUF_TOO_BIG: Provided buffer is too big (>16kB).
XST_USB_NO_DESC_AVAILABLE: No TX descriptor is available.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a data buffer from the endpoint of the given endpoint number and pass it to the application.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
is holding the buffer length.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a data buffer from the endpoint of the given endpoint number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
(OUT param) is a pointer to the buffer pointer to hold the reference of the data buffer.
(OUT param) is a pointer to the integer that will hold the buffer length.
is the opaque handle to be used when the buffer is released.

simplesect.para: After handling the data in the buffer, the user MUST release the buffer using the Handle by calling the XUsbPs_EpBufferRelease() function.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a previously received data buffer to the driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the buffer that is returned.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a data buffer from the endpoint of the given endpoint number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
(OUT param) is a pointer to the buffer pointer to hold the reference of the data buffer.
(OUT param) is a pointer to the integer that will hold the buffer length.

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, u8 EpNum, u8 Direction, XUsbPs_EpHandlerFunc CallBackFunc, void *CallBackRef)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_EpSetHandler

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler for endpoint events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.

is the direction of the endpoint (bitfield):
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_OUT
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_IN
is the Handler callback function. Can be NULL if the user wants to disable the handler entry.
is the user definable data pointer that will be passed back if the handler is called. May be NULL.

simplesect.para: The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, u8 EpNum, u8 Direction, XUsbPs_EpIsoHandlerFunc CallBackFunc)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_EpSetIsoHandler

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler for ISO endpoint events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.

is the direction of the endpoint (bitfield):
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_OUT
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_IN
is the Handler callback function. Can be NULL if the user wants to disable the handler entry.

simplesect.para: The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the XUsbPs instance.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.

is the direction of the endpoint (bitfield):
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_OUT
XUSBPS_EP_DIRECTION_IN

briefdescription.para: This function extracts the Setup Data from a given endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the number of the endpoint to receive data from.
is a pointer to the setup data structure to be filled.

memberdef.argsstring: (XUsbPs *InstancePtr, XUsbPs_DeviceConfig *CfgPtr, int EpNum, unsigned short NewDirection, int DirectionChanged)
memberdef.name: XUsbPs_ReconfigureEp

briefdescription.para: This function reconfigures one Ep corresponding to host's request of setting alternate interface.

detaileddescription.para: The endpoint has been disabled before this call.
Both QH and dTDs are updated for the new configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
Pointer to the updated XUsbPs DEVICE configuration structure.
The endpoint to be reconfigured.
The new transfer direction the endpoint.
A boolean value indicate whether the transfer direction has changed.

briefdescription.para: This function perform the reset sequence to the given usbps interface by configuring the appropriate control bits in the usbps specific registers.

detaileddescription.para:
the usbps reset sequence involves the below steps Disable the interrupts Clear the status registers Apply the reset command and wait for reset complete status Update the relevant control registers with reset values
BaseAddress
of the interface
N/A.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function handles TX buffer interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: It is called by the interrupt when a transmit complete interrupt occurs. It returns buffers of completed descriptors to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Bit mask of endpoints that caused a transmit complete interrupt.

briefdescription.para: This function handles RX buffer interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: It is called by the interrupt when a receive complete interrupt occurs. It notifies the callback functions that have been registered with the individual endpoints that data has been received.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
Bit mask of endpoints that caused a receive complete interrupt.

briefdescription.para: This function handles a RESET interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: It will notify the interrupt handler callback of the RESET condition.

InstancePtr
is pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller
IrqSts
is the Interrupt status register content. To be passed on to the user.
None None.

briefdescription.para: This function handles a Setup Packet interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: It will notify the interrupt handler callback of the RESET condition.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
None None

briefdescription.para: This function is the first-level interrupt handler for the USB core.

detaileddescription.para: All USB interrupts will be handled here. Depending on the type of the interrupt, second level interrupt handler may be called. Second level interrupt handlers will be registered by the user using the: XUsbPs_IntrSetHandler() and/or XUsbPs_EpSetHandler() functions.

parameterdescription.para: is a Reference passed to the interrupt register function. In our case this will be a pointer to the XUsbPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function registers the user callback handler for controller (non-endpoint) interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Callback function to register. CallBackFunc may be NULL to clear the entry.
is the user data reference passed to the callback function. CallBackRef may be NULL.
is the User interrupt mask. Defines which interrupts will cause the callback to be called.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE: Callback could not be registered.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the controller configuration based on the unique controller ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each controller in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified controller ID/BaseAddress was not found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceID is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value can be put into the queue head.

detaileddescription.para: The dTD describes to the device controller the location and quantity of data to be sent/received for given transfer. The driver does not attempt to modify any field in an active dTD except the Next Link Pointer.Pointer to the next descriptor

Many of the following macros modify Device Queue Head (dQH) data structures and Device Transfer Descriptor (dTD) data structures. Those structures can potentially reside in CACHED memory. Therefore, it's the callers responsibility to ensure cache coherency by using provided XUsbPs_dQHInvalidateCache() XUsbPs_dQHFlushCache() XUsbPs_dTDInvalidateCache() XUsbPs_dTDFlushCache()
function calls.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Transfer Length for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the dTD element.
is the length to be set. Range: 0..16384

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XUsbPs_dTDSetTransferLen(u32 dTDPtr, u32 Len)

briefdescription.para: This macro gets the Next Link pointer of the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Next Link pointer of the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.
is the Next Link Pointer

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XUsbPs_dTDSetTransferLen(u32 dTDPtr, u32 Len)

briefdescription.para: This macro gets the Transfer Length for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Interrupt On Complete (IOC) bit for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Terminate bit for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the Terminate bit for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the given descriptor is active.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

listitem.para: FALSE: The buffer is not active.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Active bit for the given Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the multiplier bit for the Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.
is the multiplier value.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XUsbPs_dTDSetMultO(u32 dTDPtr, u32 val)

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the content of a field in a Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dTD element.
is the field ID inside the dTD element to read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XUsbPs_ReaddTD(u32 dTDPtr, u32 Id)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes a value to a field in a Transfer Descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the dTD element.
is the field ID inside the dTD element to read.
is the value to write to the field.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XUsbPs_WritedTD(u32 dTDPtr, u32 Id, u32 Val)

detaileddescription.para: Device queue heads are arranged in an array in a continuous area of memory pointed to by the ENDPOINTLISTADDR pointer. The device controller will index into this array based upon the endpoint number received from the USB bus. All information necessary to respond to transactions for all primed transfers is contained in this list so the Device Controller can readily respond to incoming requests without having to traverse a linked list.

The device Endpoint Queue Head (dQH) is where all transfers are managed. The dQH is a 48-byte data structure, but must be aligned on a 64-byte boundary. During priming of an endpoint, the dTD (device transfer descriptor) is copied into the overlay area of the dQH, which starts at the nextTD pointer DWord and continues through the end of the buffer pointers DWords. After a transfer is complete, the dTD status DWord is updated in the dTD pointed to by the currentTD pointer. While a packet is in progress, the overlay area of the dQH is used as a staging area for the dTD so that the Device Controller can access needed information with little minimal latency.

simplesect.para: Software must ensure that no interface data structure reachable by the Device Controller spans a 4K-page boundary. The first element of the Endpoint Queue Head List must be aligned on a 4K boundary.dQH Configuration

briefdescription.para: dTD Next Link Ptr in dQH overlay
This macro sets the Maximum Packet Length field of the give Queue Head.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.
is the length to be set.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XUsbPs_dQHSetMaxPacketLen(u32 dQHPtr, u32 Len)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the Interrupt On Setup (IOS) bit for an endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the Interrupt On Setup (IOS) bit for an endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables Zero Length Termination for the endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables Zero Length Termination for the endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the content of a field in a Queue Head.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.
is the Field ID inside the dQH element to read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XUsbPs_ReaddQH(u32 dQHPtr, u32 Id)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes a value to a field in a Queue Head.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the dQH element.
is the Field ID inside the dQH element to read.
is the Value to write to the field.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XUsbPs_WritedQH(u32 dQHPtr, u32 Id, u32 Val)

briefdescription.para: Mask for All IRQ Enable masks.

detaileddescription.para: < Alignment of the Device Queue Head List BASE. Alignment of a Device Queue Head structure.

briefdescription.para: Alignment of a Device Transfer Descriptor structure.
Size of one RX buffer for a OUT Transfer Descriptor.
Maximum size of one RX/TX buffer.
Alignment requirement for Transfer Descriptor buffers.
This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address for the USB registers.
is the register offset to be read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XUsbPs_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the the base address for the USB registers.
is the register offset to be written.
is the the 32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XUsbPs_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

detaileddescription.para: The implementation of the XUsbPs driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains the functions that are related to interrupt processing for the EPB USB driver.
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
The implementation of the XUsbPs interface reset functionality.
This file contains a configuration table where each entry is a configuration structure for an XUsbPs device in the system.
This is an internal file containung the definitions for endpoints.
Endpoint specific function implementations.
Functions in this file are the minimum required functions for this driver. See xusbps.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 10/10/10 First release 2.1 kpc 04/28/14 Removed unused functions 2.5 pm 02/20/20 Added usb state interface.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 10/10/10 First release 1.03a nm 09/21/12 Fixed CR#678977. Added proper sequence for setup packet handling. 1.04a nm 11/02/12 Fixed CR#683931. Mult bits are set properly in dQH. 2.00a kpc 04/03/14 Fixed CR#777763. Updated the macro names 2.1 kpc 04/28/14 Added XUsbPs_EpBufferSendWithZLT api and merged common code to XUsbPs_EpQueueRequest. 2.3 bss 01/19/16 Modified XUsbPs_EpQueueRequest function to fix CR#873972 (moving of dTD Head/Tail Pointers)and CR#873974(invalidate Caches After Buffer Receive in Endpoint Buffer Handler...) 2.5 pm 02/20/20 Added ISO endpoint support. 2.11 ka 01/29/26 Fix codespell errors.

detaileddescription.para: It is included by the xusbps_endpoint.c which is implementing the endpoint functions and by xusbps_intr.c.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 10/10/10 First release 2.5 pm 02/20/20 Added multiplier bit for ISO frame handling.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 10/10/10 First release 2.00 hk 22/01/14 Added check to select second instance.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.05a kpc 10/10/10 first version 2.11 ka 01/29/26 Fix codespell errors.

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xusbps.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 10/10/10 First release 1.04a nm 10/23/12 Fixed CR# 679106. 1.05a kpc 07/03/13 Added XUsbPs_ResetHw function prototype 2.00a kpc 04/03/14 Fixed CR#777764. Corrected max endpoint vale and masks 2.5 pm 02/20/20 Added Endpoint Control Register bit positions for Rx & Tx

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 10/10/10 First release 1.03a nm 09/21/12 Fixed CR#678977. Added proper sequence for setup packet handling. 2.3 bss 01/19/16 Modified XUsbPs_EpQueueRequest function to fix CR#873972 (moving of dTD Head/Tail Pointers properly). 2.5 pm 02/20/20 Added ISO endpoint support.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 10/10/10 First release 2.8 pm 07/07/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 2.11 bdk 12/08/25 Updated comments to support SDT flow for Doxygen documentation.
